description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, ideal for thrusting and slashing. The blade is metallic, exhibiting a slightly worn, matte gray finish that suggests frequent use or age. The crossguard is simple and straight, providing hand protection and balance, and is also metallic with a similar finish to the blade. The handle is cylindrical, ribbed for grip, and capped with a faceted pommel, both appearing to be made of metal. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements, giving the sword a utilitarian and functional appearance. The overall proportions and design indicate it is a one-handed arming sword, commonly used in historical European contexts.
description: A straight, double-edged medieval sword with a simple crossguard, ribbed handle, and faceted pommel, all in a worn metallic finish. The design is utilitarian and lacks ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, ribbed handle, and faceted pommel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard and pommel visible as narrow edges.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, handle, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the slimness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: straight, ribbed metal handle with faceted pommel
